scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a supported constituent status request only after case-management, notice, correspondence, and privacy-release records aligned on a safe administrative summary.
Missing jurisdiction, program, case, notice, identity, and privacy prerequisites produced a blocked-state request for minimum facts instead of guesswork.
Deadline waiver and pressure requests were rejected as unsupported policy exceptions and routed to the responsible adjudication or policy owner.
Eligibility and appeal-window risk triggered due-process-sensitive escalation rather than a substituted benefits decision.
A missing permit appeal record, timestamp, and acknowledgment were handled as an audit-trail blocker requiring records or program review.
Requests to order enforcement action and confirm penalties were refused and routed to the enforcement owner.
FOIA, Privacy Act, and confidentiality conflicts were escalated to public records, privacy, or legal instead of being interpreted by the lane.
Procurement-protest communication became a clean handoff to procurement or legal with solicitation records and communication limits named explicitly.
Repeated unanswered contacts and conflicting notices were escalated as a repeat-failure complaint with a record-backed history.
The lane preserved government terminology such as casework, privacy release, benefit notice, FOIA tracking number, solicitation, COR, audit trail, and agency liaison.
Conflicting tenant policy, program guidance, and statutory notice context were recorded as a precedence conflict requiring escalation.
Tenant-specific service-level, records-retention, and equity controls were applied without discarding the public baseline, and assumptions were documented.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and government-and-public-sector abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and government-and-public-sector contract.
The package stays inside constituent-services administrative support and does not claim adjudication, enforcement, legal, procurement, public-records, privacy, or financial-approval authority.
